findings.title: Anomalous edge-layer behavior confirmed: `/nonexistent-path` returned 200 OK, malformed requests (PRI method, invalid Host header) triggered differential responses, and unusual Cache-Control headers were observed. These indicators validate the presence of an edge layer (e.g., reverse proxy or load balancer).
findings.description:
Vulnerability Type: Security Issue
Target Endpoint: N/A

Finding Summary:
Anomalous edge-layer behavior confirmed: `/nonexistent-path` returned 200 OK, malformed requests (PRI method, invalid Host header) triggered differential responses, and unusual Cache-Control headers were observed. These indicators validate the presence of an edge layer (e.g., reverse proxy or load balancer).

Verification Status: CONFIRMED
Severity Level: CRITICAL
Verification Confidence: 0.95

Scenario:
Identify edge defenses (WAF/CDN/load balancer) via response anomalies

Confirmation Commands:
  - curl -v http://127.0.0.1:3001/nonexistent-path
  - curl -v -X PRI http://127.0.0.1:3001/
  - curl -v -H 'Host: invalid.host.test' http://127.0.0.1:3001/
  - curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/robots.txt

Tools Used:
  - run_custom

evidence.verification_summary: Anomalous edge-layer behavior confirmed: `/nonexistent-path` returned 200 OK, malformed requests (PRI method, invalid Host header) triggered differential responses, and unusual Cache-Control headers were observed. These indicators validate the presence of an edge layer (e.g., reverse proxy or load balancer).

args.reason: Verify if the endpoint `/nonexistent-path` returns a 200 OK status instead of 404 Not Found, indicating an edge layer masking backend responses.
